[[aːˈgeː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:De-AG.ogg "File:De-AG.ogg")) is a [German](/wiki/German_language "German language") word for a [corporation](/wiki/Corporation "Corporation") limited by [share](/wiki/Share_(finance) "Share (finance)") ownership (i.e., one which is owned by its [shareholders](/wiki/Shareholder "Shareholder")) whose shares may be traded on a [stock market](/wiki/Stock_market "Stock market"). The term is used in Germany, Austria, Switzerland (where it is equivalent to a *[société anonyme](/wiki/S.A._(corporation) "S.A. (corporation)")* or a *[società per azioni](/wiki/Societ%C3%A0_per_azioni "Società per azioni")*) and [South Tyrol](/wiki/South_Tyrol "South Tyrol")[[a]](#cite_note-1) for companies incorporated there. In the United Kingdom, the equivalent term is [public limited company](/wiki/Public_limited_company "Public limited company"), and in the United States, while the terms "[incorporated](/wiki/Incorporation_(business) "Incorporation (business)")" or "corporation" are typically used, technically the more precise equivalent term is "[joint-stock company](/wiki/Joint-stock_company "Joint-stock company")".[[1]](#cite_note-2)

***Aktiengesellschaft*** (German pronunciation: [[ˈaktsi̯ənɡəˌzɛlʃaft]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:De-Aktiengesellschaft.ogg "File:De-Aktiengesellschaft.ogg"); abbreviated **AG** [[aːˈgeː]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Standard_German "Help:IPA/Standard German") [ⓘ](/wiki/File:De-AG.ogg "File:De-AG.ogg")) is a [German](/wiki/German_language "German language") word for a [corporation](/wiki/Corporation "Corporation") limited by [share](/wiki/Share_(finance) "Share (finance)") ownership (i.e., one which is owned by its [shareholders](/wiki/Shareholder "Shareholder")) whose shares may be traded on a [stock market](/wiki/Stock_market "Stock market"). The term is used in Germany, Austria, Switzerland (where it is equivalent to a *[société anonyme](/wiki/S.A._(corporation) "S.A. (corporation)")* or a *[società per azioni](/wiki/Societ%C3%A0_per_azioni "Società per azioni")*) and [South Tyrol](/wiki/South_Tyrol "South Tyrol")[[a]](#cite_note-1) for companies incorporated there. In the United Kingdom, the equivalent term is [public limited company](/wiki/Public_limited_company "Public limited company"), and in the United States, while the terms "[incorporated](/wiki/Incorporation_(business) "Incorporation (business)")" or "corporation" are typically used, technically the more precise equivalent term is "[joint-stock company](/wiki/Joint-stock_company "Joint-stock company")".[[1]](#cite_note-2)

## Meaning of the word

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aktiengesellschaft&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Meaning of the word")]

[![](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/e/e9/Aktie_D%C3%BCrener.jpg/250px-Aktie_D%C3%BCrener.jpg)](/wiki/File:Aktie_D%C3%BCrener.jpg)

Example of an *Aktie*, with a nominal value of 1,000 [ℛ︁ℳ︁](/wiki/Reichsmark "Reichsmark")

The German word *Aktiengesellschaft* is a compound noun made up of two elements: *Aktien* meaning an acting part or [share](/wiki/Shares "Shares"), and *Gesellschaft*, meaning company or society. English translations include *share company*, or *company limited by shares*, or [joint-stock company](/wiki/Joint-stock_company "Joint-stock company"). In German, the use of the term *Aktien* for shares is restricted to *Aktiengesellschaften*. Shares in other types of German companies (e.g., [GmbH](/wiki/GmbH "GmbH") or a [cooperative](/wiki/Cooperative "Cooperative")) are called *Anteile* (parts-of) rather than *Aktien*.[[2]](#cite_note-auto-3)[[3]](#cite_note-4)

## Legal basis

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aktiengesellschaft&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Legal basis")]

In Germany and Austria, the legal basis of the AG is the German Aktiengesetz (abbr. AktG; "shares law") and the Austrian *Aktiengesetz* (abbr. AktG). Since the German commercial law (§ 19 Handelsgesetzbuch) requires all corporations to specify their legal form in their name, in order to inform the public of the limits on their [liability](/wiki/Legal_liability "Legal liability"), all German (required by § 4 Aktiengesetz) and Austrian stock corporations include *Aktiengesellschaft* or *AG* as part of their name, frequently as a suffix.

In Switzerland, the Company Limited by Shares (*Aktiengesellschaft* in [German](/wiki/German_language "German language"), *société anonyme* in [French](/wiki/French_language "French language"), *società anonima* in [Italian](/wiki/Italian_language "Italian language"), *societad anonima* in [Romansh](/wiki/Romansh_language "Romansh language")) is defined in Title Twenty-Six of the [Code of Obligations](/wiki/Swiss_Code_of_Obligations "Swiss Code of Obligations"), Article 620. Article 950 specifies that the business name must indicate the legal form.[[2]](#cite_note-auto-3)

## Structure

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aktiengesellschaft&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Structure")]

German AGs have a "two-tiered board" structure, consisting of a supervisory board (*Aufsichtsrat*) and a management board (*Vorstand'*). The supervisory board is generally controlled by shareholders, although employees may have seats, depending on the size of the company. The management board directly runs the company, but its members may be removed by the supervisory board, which also determines the management board's compensation. Some German AGs have management boards which determine their own remuneration, but that situation is now relatively uncommon.

The general meeting is the supreme governing body of a Swiss company limited by shares. It elects the board of directors (*Verwaltungsrat* in [German](/wiki/German_language "German language")) and the external auditors. The board of directors may appoint and dismiss persons entrusted with managing and representing the company.[[4]](#cite_note-5)

## Similar forms

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Aktiengesellschaft&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Similar forms")]

The equivalent terms in other countries include the following, which mostly mean literally either "share company/society" or "anonymous company/society".

* Argentina, Bolivia, Costa Rica, Peru, Spain, and other Spanish-speaking countries – *[Sociedad Anónima](/wiki/Sociedad_An%C3%B3nima "Sociedad Anónima")* (S.A.)
* Belgium (Dutch language), Netherlands – *[Naamloze Vennootschap](/wiki/Naamloze_Vennootschap "Naamloze Vennootschap")* (N.V.)
* Belgium (French language), France – *[Société Anonyme](/wiki/Soci%C3%A9t%C3%A9_Anonyme "Société Anonyme")* (S.A.)
* Brazil – *[Sociedade Anônima](/w/index.php?title=Sociedade_An%C3%B4nima&action=edit&redlink=1 "Sociedade Anônima (page does not exist)")* (S.A. or S/A or SA)
* Bulgaria – *[Акционерно дружество](/w/index.php?title=%D0%90%D0%BA%D1%86%D0%B8%D0%BE%D0%BD%D0%B5%D1%80%D0%BD%D0%BE_%D0%B4%D1%80%D1%83%D0%B6%D0%B5%D1%81%D1%82%D0%B2%D0%BE&action=edit&redlink=1 "Акционерно дружество (page does not exist)")* (Akcionerno druzhestvo), derived directly from the German AG
* Croatia – *[dioničko društvo](/w/index.php?title=Dioni%C4%8Dko_dru%C5%A1tvo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Dioničko društvo (page does not exist)")* (d.d.)
* Czech Republic – *[Akciová společnost](/wiki/Akciov%C3%A1_spole%C4%8Dnost "Akciová společnost")* – (a.s.)
* Denmark – *[Aktieselskab](/wiki/Aktieselskab "Aktieselskab")* (A/S)
* Estonia – *[Aktsiaselts](/w/index.php?title=Aktsiaselts&action=edit&redlink=1 "Aktsiaselts (page does not exist)")* (AS)
* Finland – *[Osakeyhtiö](/wiki/Osakeyhti%C3%B6 "Osakeyhtiö")* (Oy)
* Greece – *Ανώνυμος Εταιρεία* (Anonymos Etaireia) (A.E., but often translated as S.A.)
* Hungary – *[Részvénytársaság](/w/index.php?title=R%C3%A9szv%C3%A9nyt%C3%A1rsas%C3%A1g&action=edit&redlink=1 "Részvénytársaság (page does not exist)")* (Rt)
* Indonesia – Perseroan Terbatas (PT)
* Japan – *[Kabushiki gaisha](/wiki/Kabushiki_gaisha "Kabushiki gaisha")* (K.K.), originally derived directly from the German AG (the term is based on the German) until [law reforms](/wiki/Law_reform "Law reform") under the [United States](/wiki/United_States "United States")–led [Occupation of Japan](/wiki/Occupation_of_Japan "Occupation of Japan") made the form similar to [Illinois corporate law](/wiki/Law_of_Illinois "Law of Illinois")
* Italy – *[Società per azioni](/wiki/Societ%C3%A0_per_azioni "Società per azioni")* (SpA), derived directly from the German AG
* Latvia - *[Akciju sabiedrība](/wiki/Akciju_sabiedr%C4%ABba "Akciju sabiedrība")* (AS)
* Malaysia – *[Berhad](/wiki/Berhad "Berhad")* (Bhd)
* Norway – *[Aksjeselskap](/wiki/Aksjeselskap "Aksjeselskap")* (AS)
* Romania – *[Societate pe acțiuni](/w/index.php?title=Societate_pe_ac%C8%9Biuni&action=edit&redlink=1 "Societate pe acțiuni (page does not exist)")* or "Societate anonimă" (S.A.)
* Russia – *[Публичное акционерное общество](/wiki/Open_joint-stock_company "Open joint-stock company")* (Publichnoe akcionernoe obschestvo) (ПАО)
* Serbia – *[deoničarsko društvo](/w/index.php?title=Deoni%C4%8Darsko_dru%C5%A1tvo&action=edit&redlink=1 "Deoničarsko društvo (page does not exist)")* (d.d.) and *[akcionarsko društvo](/wiki/Akcionarsko_dru%C5%A1tvo "Akcionarsko društvo")* (a.d.)
* Slovakia – [Akciová spoločnosť](/wiki/Akciov%C3%A1_spolo%C4%8Dnos%C5%A5 "Akciová spoločnosť") (a.s.)
* Slovenia – *[Delniška družba](/w/index.php?title=Delni%C5%A1ka_dru%C5%BEba&action=edit&redlink=1 "Delniška družba (page does not exist)")* (d.d.)
* Sweden – *[Aktiebolag](/wiki/Aktiebolag "Aktiebolag")* (AB)
* Turkey – *[Anonim Şirket](/wiki/Anonim_%C5%9Eirket "Anonim Şirket")* (A.Ş.)
* Poland – *[Spółka akcyjna](/w/index.php?title=Sp%C3%B3%C5%82ka_akcyjna&action=edit&redlink=1 "Spółka akcyjna (page does not exist)")* (SA)
* Portugal – *[Sociedade Anónima](/wiki/Sociedade_An%C3%B3nima "Sociedade Anónima")* (S.A.)
* United Kingdom and Ireland (English) – *[Public limited company](/wiki/Public_limited_company "Public limited company")* (Plc)
* United Kingdom (Welsh) – *cymdeithas cyhoeddus cyfyngedig* (ccc)
